name: atmst

metadata.name: atmst
metadata.summary: A Python library for wrangling atproto-flavoured Merkle Search Trees

documentation:
# atmst

[![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/license-MIT-blue)](./LICENSE) ![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/works%20on%20my%20machine-forestgreen) ![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/test%20coverage-nonzero-red) [![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/docs-exist!-orange)](https://davidbuchanan314.github.io/atmst/) ![Static Badge](https://img.shields.io/badge/cryptography-certified%20hand--rolled-yellow)

A Python library for wrangling atproto-flavoured Merkle Search Trees

Current status: ⚠️ prototype ⚠️

### Installation

```
git clone https://github.com/DavidBuchanan314/atmst
cd atmst
python3 -m pip install .
```

dev install: (editable)

```
python3 -m pip install -e .
```

Running the tests:

```
python3 -m unittest discover -v
```

build the docs:

```
cd docs/
sphinx-apidoc -f -o _apidocs/ ../src/atmst ../src/atmst/all.py
make html
# open _build/html/index.html
```

publishing to pypi: (this one is mainly for my benefit!)

```
python3 -m build
python3 -m twine upload --repository pypi dist/*
```

functions.name: atmst.util.indent
documentation: Indent a multi-line string

modules.name: atmst.all
documentation:
This module collects "all" the commonly needed imports into a single import.
It might be convenient. I might get rid of it.

classes.name: atmst.blockstore.BlockStore
documentation:
A block store is a k/v store where values are immutable once set. They can be deleted, though.
In practice, k==hash(v), but this API doesn't care about that.

I'm not using the "native" __getitem__, __setitem__, __del__ methods because
the semantics of these methods differ subtly.

if you call put() twice with the same args, the second call is a nop.
if you call put() twice with the same key but different value, you get a ValueError

get() offers no default return value, you get a KeyError if it doesn't exist.

if you try to delete a key that doesn't exist, that's a nop.

methods.name: atmst.blockstore.MemoryBlockStore.__init__
documentation: NB: if a state dict is passed, it'll get mutated in-place

classes.name: atmst.blockstore.SqliteBlockStore
documentation:
NB: Caller is responsible for calling commit(), etc.
TODO: consider allowing a custom table name?

classes.name: atmst.blockstore.OverlayBlockStore
documentation:
reads come from "upper", then "lower" if they don't exist in upper.
writes/deletes go only to "upper".

functions.name: atmst.blockstore.car_file.decode_varint
documentation: should be equivalent to multiformats.varint.decode(), but not extremely slow for no reason.

classes.name: atmst.blockstore.car_file.ReadOnlyCARBlockStore

methods.name: atmst.blockstore.car_file.ReadOnlyCARBlockStore.__init__
documentation: pre-scan over the whole file, recording the offsets of each block

class_variables.name: atmst.blockstore.car_file.ReadOnlyCARBlockStore.block_offsets
documentation: CID -> (offset, length)

This is a sliiiightly unclean abstraction because BlockStores are indexed
by `bytes` rather than CID, but same idea. This is convenient for verifying
proofs provided in CAR format, and for testing.

functions.name: atmst.mst.diff.record_diff
documentation:
Given two sets of MST nodes (for example, the result of {meth}`mst_diff`), this
returns an iterator of record changes.

functions.name: atmst.mst.diff.very_slow_mst_diff
documentation:
This should return the same result as {meth}`mst_diff`, but it gets there in a slow
but much more obvious way (enumerating all nodes), so it's useful for testing.

It's actually faster for smaller trees, but it chokes on trees with thousands of nodes (especially if the NodeStore is slow).

functions.name: atmst.mst.diff.mst_diff
documentation:
XXX: This implementation is not yet ready for prime-time!

Given two MST root node CIDs, efficiently compute the difference between the two trees. The result is two sets, holding the created and deleted MST nodes respectively (referenced by CIDs).

functions.name: atmst.mst.node_wrangler._tuple_replace_at
documentation: tuple helpers

classes.name: atmst.mst.node_wrangler.NodeWrangler

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_wrangler.NodeWrangler._squash_top
documentation: strip empty nodes from the top of the tree

NodeWrangler is where core MST transformation ops are implemented, backed
by a NodeStore

The external APIs take a CID (the MST root) and return a CID (the new root),
while storing any newly created nodes in the NodeStore.

Neither method should ever fail - deleting a node that doesn't exist is a nop,
and adding the same node twice with the same value is also a nop. Callers
can detect these cases by seeing if the initial and final CIDs changed.

classes.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.next_kv
documentation: everything above here is core tree walking logic
everything below here is helper functions

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.iter_kv
documentation: iterate over every k/v pair in key-sorted order
NB: should really be p/v standing for path/value

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.iter_nodes
documentation: get all mst nodes down and to the right of the current position

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.iter_kv_range
documentation: start inclusive

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.PATH_MIN
documentation: string that compares less than all legal path strings

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.PATH_MAX
documentation: string that compares greater than all legal path strings

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node_walker.NodeWalker.StackFrame.node
documentation: could store CIDs only to save memory, in theory, but not much point

NodeWalker makes implementing tree diffing and other MST query ops more
convenient (but it does not, itself, implement them).

A NodeWalker starts off at the root of a tree, and can walk along or recurse
down into subtrees.

Walking "off the end" of a subtree brings you back up to its next non-empty parent.

Recall MSTNode layout:

```
keys:  (lpath)  (0,    1,    2,    3)  (rpath)
vals:           (0,    1,    2,    3)
subtrees:    (0,    1,    2,    3,    4)
```

classes.name: atmst.mst.node_store.NodeStore

methods.name: atmst.mst.node_store.NodeStore.pretty
documentation: MST pretty-printing
this should maybe not be implemented here

NodeStore wraps a BlockStore to provide a more ergonomic interface
for loading and storing MSTNodes

find the index of the first key greater than or equal to the specified key
if all keys are smaller, it returns len(keys)

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node.MSTNode.keys
documentation: collection/rkey

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node.MSTNode.vals
documentation: record CIDs

class_variables.name: atmst.mst.node.MSTNode.subtrees
documentation: a None value represents an empty subtree

k/v pairs are interleaved between subtrees like so:

```
keys:          (0,    1,    2,    3)
vals:          (0,    1,    2,    3)
subtrees:   (0,    1,    2,    3,    4)
```

If a method is implemented in this class, it's because it's a function/property
of a single node, as opposed to a whole tree
